content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission,
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

May 11, 1984


Maeley L. Tom
Chief Administrative Officer
of the Assembly
State Capitol, Room 3016
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-84-107

Dear Ms. Tom:

This letter is sent to confirm advice I gave to Doug Kim of
your office in response to your letter.

You asked whether a member of the Legislature, after he or
she has filed a declaration of candidacy, may prepare and
distribute at public expense more than 200 pieces of identical
literature by means other than the U.S. mail.

This question involves the prohibition contained in
Government Code Section 89001 which reads:

No legislative newsletter or other mass mailing
shall be sent at public expense by or on behalf of any
elected state officer after the elected state officer
has filed a declaration of candidacy for any office.

I advised Mr. Kim that this prohibition as it has been
interpreted does not apply to newsletters or other mass mailings
which are not sent through the mails. However, we are
considering possible amendments to the regulation which will
change this interpretation of Section 89001; it is highly
unlikely though that the regulation will be amended this year.

If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to
contact me.

Sincerely,

Diane Maura Fishburn
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

DMF:plh

MEMBERS
LEON PLANE
WILLIAM FILANTE
PHILLIP SARBANES
ROBERT W. NAYLOR
DON ROGERS
MIKE ROOS
STAN STATHAM
MAXINE WATERS

CHIEF ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICER
MAELEY L. TOM

EXECUTIVE SECRETARY
JULIE ANGELIDES

California Legislature

Assembly Rules Committee

ROOM 3016 · STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE: (916) 445-8173

LOUIS J. PAPAN
CHAIRMAN

April 25, 1984

Ms. Barbara Milman
Chief Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Milman:

After a Member of the Legislature has filed his or her declaration of
candidacy, without violating Section 89001 of the Government Code, may he
or she prepare and distribute at public expense more than 200 pieces of
identical literature by means other than the U.S. mail?

If you have any questions, please contact Doug Kim, Deputy Administrative
Officer at 5-7995.

I would appreciate your earliest reply.

Sincerely,

MAELEY L. TOM
Chief Administrative Officer
of the Assembly

MLT:gy

cc: Doug Kim

sections.question: After a Member of the Legislature has filed his or her declaration of candidacy, without violating Section 89001 of the Government Code, may he or she prepare and distribute at public expense more than 200 pieces of identical literature by means other than the U.S. mail?
sections.conclusion: This prohibition as it has been interpreted does not apply to newsletters or other mass mailings which are not sent through the mails. However, we are considering possible amendments to the regulation which will change this interpretation of Section 89001; it is highly unlikely though that the regulation will be amended this year.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether legislative members may prepare and distribute more than 200 pieces of identical literature at public expense through non-mail means after filing a declaration of candidacy, consistent with Government Code Section 89001.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that Government Code Section 89001's prohibition on public expense mass mailings does not currently apply to identical literature distributed through non-mail means, though potential regulatory amendments may change this interpretation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request (April 25, 1984 letter from Maeley Tom) and FPPC response (May 11, 1984 letter from Staff Counsel). Clear question and conclusion sections present. File number A-84-107 referenced.

CONCLUSION: This prohibition as it has been interpreted does not apply to newsletters or other mass mailings which are not sent through the mails. However, we are considering possible amendments to the regulation which will change this interpretation of Section 89001; it is highly unlikely though that the regulation will be amended this year.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that Government Code Section 89001's prohibition on public expense legislative mass mailings does not apply to literature distributed by means other than U.S. mail, though regulatory amendments are under consideration.
